messages.content:
You are a provider knowledge node in a distributed consensus network. You are not a dedicated summarizer or dedicated reviewer role; you are a domain provider that performs answer, synthesis, and grading using your domain knowledge.
Global rules:
- Output must be one valid JSON object only; no markdown.
- Use UNKNOWN when uncertain; do not guess.
- If a request is outside your scope, abstain instead of improvising.
- Prefer a few high-confidence facts over many weak claims.
- FAIL only on clear, concrete errors or contradictions.
- Do not invent point_id values; only use IDs provided by client.
- Be concise and technically precise.
Domain: coding_debugging.
Scope: Python, JavaScript, APIs, debugging workflow, edge-case analysis.
Working style: pragmatic bug-fixer with concrete implementation steps.

TASK: ANSWER
Return EXACT JSON schema:
{
  "abstain": true|false,
  "points": [{"text": "string"}]
}
Rules:
- Return ONLY high-confidence points inside your declared domain scope.
- If the query is mostly outside your scope, set abstain=true and return {"points": []}.
- If abstain=true, points must be empty.
- Each point must be atomic and <= 160 chars.
- Do not add generic cross-domain filler.
Input JSON:
{"query": "We use WebSockets for real-time updates. Clients reconnect often and sometimes receive duplicate or out-of-order events. We also worry about replayed tokens. How should we design ordering/deduplication and secure the channel?", "input_text": ""}

raw_content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Assign a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event per client; drop any received with a lower or duplicate number."},{"text":"Include a per‑connection token (e.g., JWT with short TTL) in every WebSocket frame and verify it server‑side to prevent replay."},{"text":"On reconnect, have the client send the last sequence it processed; the server resumes from the next number, avoiding gaps."},{"text":"Use idempotency keys (e.g., event UUID) and a short‑term cache to discard duplicate events arriving out of order."},{"text":"Implement ACK/NACK flow: client acknowledges received seq numbers; server can retransmit missing ranges on request."},{"text":"Protect tokens with HMAC‑signed timestamps; reject messages with timestamps outside an acceptable window to stop replay attacks."}]}

message.content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Assign a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event per client; drop any received with a lower or duplicate number."},{"text":"Include a per‑connection token (e.g., JWT with short TTL) in every WebSocket frame and verify it server‑side to prevent replay."},{"text":"On reconnect, have the client send the last sequence it processed; the server resumes from the next number, avoiding gaps."},{"text":"Use idempotency keys (e.g., event UUID) and a short‑term cache to discard duplicate events arriving out of order."},{"text":"Implement ACK/NACK flow: client acknowledges received seq numbers; server can retransmit missing ranges on request."},{"text":"Protect tokens with HMAC‑signed timestamps; reject messages with timestamps outside an acceptable window to stop replay attacks."}]}

raw_content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Assign each event a monotonically increasing sequence number per client session."},{"text":"Store the last processed sequence number on client and server; drop any event with a lower or equal number."},{"text":"When a client reconnects, include its last processed sequence number so the server can resend missing events."},{"text":"Use a short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) with a nonce and expiration; verify it on each WebSocket upgrade."},{"text":"Bind the token to the client’s IP or TLS session (token binding) to prevent replay from other hosts."},{"text":"Enable TLS (wss://) to protect confidentiality and integrity of the WebSocket frames."},{"text":"Optionally add a per‑message HMAC using a shared secret to detect tampering if TLS termination is off‑loaded."},{"text":"Implement a server‑side deduplication cache keyed by (client_id, sequence_id) for a limited window to handle duplicates."}]}

message.content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Assign each event a monotonically increasing sequence number per client session."},{"text":"Store the last processed sequence number on client and server; drop any event with a lower or equal number."},{"text":"When a client reconnects, include its last processed sequence number so the server can resend missing events."},{"text":"Use a short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) with a nonce and expiration; verify it on each WebSocket upgrade."},{"text":"Bind the token to the client’s IP or TLS session (token binding) to prevent replay from other hosts."},{"text":"Enable TLS (wss://) to protect confidentiality and integrity of the WebSocket frames."},{"text":"Optionally add a per‑message HMAC using a shared secret to detect tampering if TLS termination is off‑loaded."},{"text":"Implement a server‑side deduplication cache keyed by (client_id, sequence_id) for a limited window to handle duplicates."}]}

raw_content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Assign each message a monotonically increasing sequence number per client and drop any message with a lower number."},{"text":"Include a server‑generated unique message ID and keep a short‑term cache to discard duplicates."},{"text":"Use a signed token (e.g., JWT) with a short expiration and a per‑connection nonce to prevent replay."},{"text":"Require TLS (wss://) for all WebSocket traffic and verify the Origin header to restrict hosts."},{"text":"Validate the token on every new WebSocket upgrade and reject connections with missing or expired tokens."},{"text":"Implement a sliding window of acceptable sequence numbers to tolerate out‑of‑order delivery while still detecting gaps."},{"text":"Make message handling idempotent so that processing a duplicate does not cause side effects."},{"text":"Rotate signing keys regularly and revoke compromised tokens via a revocation list checked on each message."}]}

message.content: {"abstain":false,"points":[{"text":"Assign each message a monotonically increasing sequence number per client and drop any message with a lower number."},{"text":"Include a server‑generated unique message ID and keep a short‑term cache to discard duplicates."},{"text":"Use a signed token (e.g., JWT) with a short expiration and a per‑connection nonce to prevent replay."},{"text":"Require TLS (wss://) for all WebSocket traffic and verify the Origin header to restrict hosts."},{"text":"Validate the token on every new WebSocket upgrade and reject connections with missing or expired tokens."},{"text":"Implement a sliding window of acceptable sequence numbers to tolerate out‑of‑order delivery while still detecting gaps."},{"text":"Make message handling idempotent so that processing a duplicate does not cause side effects."},{"text":"Rotate signing keys regularly and revoke compromised tokens via a revocation list checked on each message."}]}

drop any event with a lower or equal number.", "N3:2:b982cee3": "When a client reconnects, include its last processed sequence number so the server can resend missing events.", "N3:3:7d28f2f1": "Use a short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) with a nonce and expiration; raw_content:
{
  "synthesis_text": "Design the WebSocket protocol with a per‑client monotonically increasing sequence number (N1:0, N3:0). The client stores the last processed sequence and includes it on reconnect so the server can resume from the next number (N1:2, N3:2). Both sides acknowledge received ranges; the client sends ACKs and can request NACKs for missing gaps, allowing the server to retransmit (N1:4). To handle out‑of‑order or duplicate frames, keep a short‑term deduplication cache keyed by (client_id, sequence_id) and discard any event with a lower or equal sequence number (N1:0, N3:1, N3:7). Additionally, attach an idempotency key such as a UUID to each event and drop duplicates using the same cache (N1:3). Secure the channel by requiring TLS (wss://) (N3:5) and using a short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) with a per‑connection nonce and timestamp on every frame (N1:1, N5:2). Verify the token on each upgrade and on every message, rejecting those with missing, expired, or out‑of‑window timestamps (N1:5). Bind the token to the client’s IP or TLS session to prevent replay from other hosts (N3:4). Optionally add a per‑message HMAC using a shared secret for extra integrity if TLS termination is off‑loaded (N3:6). This combination ensures ordered delivery, deduplication of duplicates/out‑of‑order events, reliable reconnection, and protection against token replay attacks.",
  "used_points": [
    "N1:0",
    "N1:1",
    "N1:2",
    "N1:3",
    "N1:4",
    "N1:5",
    "N3:0",
    "N3:1",
    "N3:2",
    "N3:3",
    "N3:4",
    "N3:5",
    "N3:7"
  ]
}

The client stores the last processed sequence and includes it on reconnect so the server can resume from the next number (N1:2, N3:2). Both sides acknowledge received ranges; the client sends ACKs and can request NACKs for missing gaps, allowing the server to retransmit (N1:4). To handle out‑of‑order or duplicate frames, keep a short‑term deduplication cache keyed by (client_id, sequence_id) and discard any event with a lower or equal sequence number (N1:0, N3:1, N3:7). Additionally, attach an idempotency key such as a UUID to each event and drop duplicates using the same cache (N1:3). Secure the channel by requiring TLS (wss://) (N3:5) and using a short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) with a per‑connection nonce and timestamp on every frame (N1:1, N5:2). Verify the token on each upgrade and on every message, rejecting those with missing, expired, or out‑of‑window timestamps (N1:5). Bind the token to the client’s IP or TLS session to prevent replay from other hosts (N3:4). Optionally add a per‑message HMAC using a shared secret for extra integrity if TLS termination is off‑loaded (N3:6). This combination ensures ordered delivery, deduplication of duplicates/out‑of‑order events, reliable reconnection, and protection against token replay attacks.",
  "used_points": [
    "N1:0",
    "N1:1",
    "N1:2",
    "N1:3",
    "N1:4",
    "N1:5",
    "N3:0",

    "N3:2",
    "N3:3",
    "N3:4",

    "N3:7"
  ]
}

How should we design ordering/deduplication and secure the channel?", "input_text": "", "points_map": {"N1:0:6128c764": "Assign a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event per client; drop any received with a lower or duplicate number.", "N1:1:b40c465a": "Include a per‑connection token (e.g., JWT with short TTL) in every WebSocket frame and verify it server‑side to prevent replay.", "N1:2:f3cfd0b6": "On reconnect, have the client send the last sequence it processed; drop any event with a lower or equal number.", "N3:2:b982cee3": "When a client reconnects, include its last processed sequence number so the server can resend missing events.", "N3:3:7d28f2f1": "Use a short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) with a nonce and expiration; verify it on each WebSocket upgrade.", "N3:4:bcdb1541": "Bind the token to the client’s IP or TLS session (token binding) to prevent replay from other hosts.", "N3:5:bcb721ee": "Enable TLS (wss://) to protect confidentiality and integrity of the WebSocket frames.", "N3:6:8156ab8a": "Optionally add a per‑message HMAC using a shared secret to detect tampering if TLS termination is off‑loaded.", "N3:7:bff92265": "Implement a server‑side deduplication cache keyed by (client_id, sequence_id) for a limited window to handle duplicates.", "N5:0:e3a92266": "Assign each message a monotonically increasing sequence number per client and drop any message with a lower number.", "N5:1:eaa8318b": "Include a server‑generated unique message ID and keep a short‑term cache to discard duplicates.", "N5:2:57555f13": "Use a signed token (e.g., JWT) with a short expiration and a per‑connection nonce to prevent replay.", "N5:3:95f3e195": "Require TLS (wss://) for all WebSocket traffic and verify the Origin header to restrict hosts.", "N5:4:ca686e53": "Validate the token on every new WebSocket upgrade and reject connections with missing or expired tokens.", "N5:5:ba3baea6": "Implement a sliding window of acceptable sequence numbers to tolerate out‑of‑order delivery while still detecting gaps.", "N5:6:e8196072": "Make message handling idempotent so that processing a duplicate does not cause side effects.", "N5:7:a164bc30": "Rotate signing keys regularly and revoke compromised tokens via a revocation list checked on each message."}}

{
  "synthesis_text": "Use a per‑client monotonically increasing sequence number for every event (N1:0:6128c764). The client stores the last sequence it processed and includes it on reconnect so the server can resume from the next number (N1:2:f3cfd0b6). The server acknowledges received sequences; missing ranges can be requested via ACK/NACK (N1:4:152de33d). To tolerate out‑of‑order delivery, keep a short sliding window and drop any event with a lower or duplicate sequence (N5:5:ba3baea6). Additionally, attach an idempotency key or UUID to each event and cache recent keys to discard duplicates arriving out of order (N1:3:838ad943). Secure the WebSocket channel with TLS (wss://) (N3:5:bcb721ee) and require a short‑lived, HMAC‑signed JWT that includes a nonce and timestamp on every frame (N1:1:b40c465a, N1:5:a51a77bf). Verify the token on each upgrade, bind it to the client’s IP or TLS session, and reject messages with timestamps outside an acceptable window to prevent replay (N1:5:a51a77bf). Rotate signing keys regularly and check a revocation list on each message (N5:7:a164bc30).",

}

The client stores the last processed sequence and includes it on reconnect so the server can resume from the next number (N1:2, N3:2). Both sides acknowledge received ranges; the client sends ACKs and can request NACKs for missing gaps, allowing the server to retransmit (N1:4). Additionally, attach an idempotency key such as a UUID to each event and drop duplicates using the same cache (N1:3). Secure the channel by requiring TLS (wss://) (N3:5) and using a short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) with a per‑connection nonce and timestamp on every frame (N1:1, N5:2). Verify the token on each upgrade and on every message, rejecting those with missing, expired, or out‑of‑window timestamps (N1:5). Bind the token to the client’s IP or TLS session to prevent replay from other hosts (N3:4). Optionally add a per‑message HMAC using a shared secret for extra integrity if TLS termination is off‑loaded (N3:6). Both sides acknowledge received ranges; the client sends ACKs and can request NACKs for missing gaps, allowing the server to retransmit (N1:4). To handle out‑of‑order or duplicate frames, keep a short‑term deduplication cache keyed by (client_id, sequence_id) and discard any event with a lower or equal sequence number (N1:0, N3:1, N3:7). Additionally, attach an idempotency key such as a UUID to each event and drop duplicates using the same cache (N1:3). Secure the channel by requiring TLS (wss://) (N3:5) and using a short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) with a per‑connection nonce and timestamp on every frame (N1:1, N5:2). Verify the token on each upgrade and on every message, rejecting those with missing, expired, or out‑of‑window timestamps (N1:5). Bind the token to the client’s IP or TLS session to prevent replay from other hosts (N3:4). Optionally add a per‑message HMAC using a shared secret for extra integrity if TLS termination is off‑loaded (N3:6). This combination ensures ordered delivery, deduplication of duplicates/out‑of‑order events, reliable reconnection, and protection against token replay attacks.", "target_used_points": []}, {"target_id": "N2", "target_draft": "UNKNOWN", "target_used_points": []}, {"target_id": "N3", "target_draft": "Use a per‑client monotonically increasing sequence number for each event (N1:0:6128c764) and have the client include the last processed sequence on reconnect so the server can resume from the next number (N1:2:f3cfd0b6). The server should keep the last acknowledged sequence and drop any event with a lower or duplicate number (N1:0:6128c764, N1:4:152de33d). Implement an ACK/NACK flow where the client acknowledges received ranges, allowing the server to retransmit missing gaps (N1:4:152de33d). To handle out‑of‑order duplicates, attach an idempotency key or UUID to each event and cache recent keys for a short window to discard repeats (N1:3:838ad943, N3:7:bff92265). Secure the channel by requiring TLS (wss://) (N3:5:bcb721ee) and using a short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) with a per‑connection nonce on every frame (N1:1:b40c465a). Verify the token on each upgrade, bind it to the client’s IP or TLS session to prevent replay from other hosts (N3:4:bcdb1541), and protect it with HMAC‑signed timestamps, rejecting messages outside an acceptable time window (N1:5:a51a77bf).", "target_used_points": ["N1:0:6128c764", "N1:1:b40c465a", "N1:2:f3cfd0b6", "N1:3:838ad943", "N1:4:152de33d", "N1:5:a51a77bf", "N3:4:bcdb1541", "N3:5:bcb721ee", "N3:7:bff92265"]}, {"target_id": "N4", "target_draft": "UNKNOWN", "target_used_points": []}], "points_map": {"N1:0:6128c764": "Assign a monotonically increasing sequence number to each event per client; drop any received with a lower or duplicate number.", "N1:1:b40c465a": "Include a per‑connection token (e.g., JWT with short TTL) in every WebSocket frame and verify it server‑side to prevent replay.", "N1:2:f3cfd0b6": "On reconnect, have the client send the last sequence it processed; the server resumes from the next number, avoiding gaps.", "N1:3:838ad943": "Use idempotency keys (e.g., event UUID) and a short‑term cache to discard duplicate events arriving out of order.", "N1:4:152de33d": "Implement ACK/NACK flow: client acknowledges received seq numbers; server can retransmit missing ranges on request.", "N1:5:a51a77bf": "Protect tokens with HMAC‑signed timestamps; reject messages with timestamps outside an acceptable window to stop replay attacks.", "N3:0:c1ed6f60": "Assign each event a monotonically increasing sequence number per client session.", "N3:1:8943c261": "Store the last processed sequence number on client and server; drop any event with a lower or equal number.", "N3:2:b982cee3": "When a client reconnects, include its last processed sequence number so the server can resend missing events.", "N3:3:7d28f2f1": "Use a short‑lived signed token (e.g., JWT) with a nonce and expiration; verify it on each WebSocket upgrade.", "N3:4:bcdb1541": "Bind the token to the client’s IP or TLS session (token binding) to prevent replay from other hosts.", "N3:5:bcb721ee": "Enable TLS (wss://) to protect confidentiality and integrity of the WebSocket frames.", "N3:6:8156ab8a": "Optionally add a per‑message HMAC using a shared secret to detect tampering if TLS termination is off‑loaded.", "N3:7:bff92265": "Implement a server‑side deduplication cache keyed by (client_id, sequence_id) for a limited window to handle duplicates.", "N5:0:e3a92266": "Assign each message a monotonically increasing sequence number per client and drop any message with a lower number.", "N5:1:eaa8318b": "Include a server‑generated unique message ID and keep a short‑term cache to discard duplicates.", "N5:2:57555f13": "Use a signed token (e.g., JWT) with a short expiration and a per‑connection nonce to prevent replay.", "N5:3:95f3e195": "Require TLS (wss://) for all WebSocket traffic and verify the Origin header to restrict hosts.", "N5:4:ca686e53": "Validate the token on every new WebSocket upgrade and reject connections with missing or expired tokens.", "N5:5:ba3baea6": "Implement a sliding window of acceptable sequence numbers to tolerate out‑of‑order delivery while still detecting gaps.", "N5:6:e8196072": "Make message handling idempotent so that processing a duplicate does not cause side effects.", "N5:7:a164bc30": "Rotate signing keys regularly and revoke compromised tokens via a revocation list checked on each message."}}
